description: Replace sequential agent dispatch with asyncio.gather in both execute() and execute_streaming() multi-agent paths, and add tests verifying parallel behavior.
steps: In backend/app/agents/orchestrator.py execute() method: Replace the sequential for-loop (around line 152-175) that iterates over routing.get('tasks', []) with parallel dispatch using asyncio.gather(). Send all status callbacks before the gather call. Collect results from gather.

In backend/app/agents/orchestrator.py execute_streaming() method: Replace the sequential for-loop in the 'Multi-agent routing -- fall back to non-streaming' section (around line 413-435) with the same asyncio.gather pattern. Send all status callbacks before gather.
Ensure the pending_action extraction, _consolidate_responses, and _suggest_followup logic continues to work unchanged with the gathered results list.
Do NOT modify single-agent dispatch paths (they're already optimized and streaming-capable).
Do NOT modify the _handle_pending multi-intent loop (it has sequential dependency on pending resolution).

Add a test in backend/tests/test_agents.py (or a new test file) that verifies multi-agent dispatch runs concurrently: register two mock agents that each sleep/delay, dispatch to both, and assert total wall-clock time is closer to 1x delay than 2x delay (proving parallelism).
Add a test that verifies all status callbacks are still emitted for each agent even with parallel dispatch.
Run the existing test suite (python -m pytest tests/ -v from backend/) to confirm nothing is broken.
Commit the changes with message 'feat: parallel agent dispatch with asyncio.gather for multi-intent queries'.
acceptance_criteria: Multi-agent dispatch in execute() uses asyncio.gather instead of sequential awaits
Multi-agent dispatch in execute_streaming() uses asyncio.gather instead of sequential awaits
Status callbacks are emitted for each agent before dispatch
All existing tests in test_agents.py pass
New test proves parallel execution (wall-clock time assertion)
New test proves status callbacks still fire for all agents
